text.title: Churn Prevention Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every application of `Churn Prevention` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Prevention MUST follow three phases: early intervention (pre-decision) -> save offer (in cancel flow) -> win-back (post-cancel). Discount-only blasts at random users are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Discount sprays kill margin without addressing reason. Phased approach targets users at the moment of maximum intent + minimum cost-to-save.

rule.statement: Save offers MUST be matched to cancellation reason segment, not a generic flow. Minimum: price-objection, missing-feature, low-usage, competitor-switch, lifecycle-end (>= 5 segments).
rule.rationale: A 'too expensive' user wants a discount or downgrade; a 'missing feature' user wants a roadmap commitment or refund. Same offer to both loses both.

rule.statement: Each save offer MUST have a documented unit-economics cap: max discount × months <= 50% of LTV of the saved customer. Offers above the cap require sign-off.

rationale.source: SaaS finance / Profitwell cohort LTV math

rule.rationale: Unbounded save offers are a worse outcome than the original churn — you save the logo but lose the unit economics. Cap forces a deliberate tradeoff.

rule.statement: Win-back campaigns MUST run within 30-90 days of cancel. Beyond 90 days conversion drops > 70% based on Iterable / Klaviyo benchmarks.
rule.rationale: Recency matters. The user still remembers the product; the alternative is still imperfect; the unsubscribe wound is still warm. After 90 days they're cold leads, not win-back.

rule.statement: Playbook MUST define a stop-loss: if blended save-offer margin drops below 30% gross over a 4-week window, pause the offer and re-segment.
rule.rationale: Save offers can scale unprofitable retention. Without a stop-loss the playbook silently turns into a discount factory.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies If fail OR any item in Skip If triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
